body_txt: Sometimes in cartoons, a character may say the same line over and over, with the same recording of the same line. There is a variation in which two characters may get into an argument ( Duck Season, Rabbit Season ) and use the same sound clip. Sometimes, the original dialogue tracks are lost, because they're distorted beyond usability, or because the source recordings were destroyed or otherwise disappeared. Other times, non-dialogue tracks are often recorded in separate sessions to avoid overstraining. This is especially common for screams. Screams strain the human voice, and repeated screaming can lead to Inopportune Voice Cracking . This, in turn, leads dialogue editors to use the best-performed takes more than once. And for characters such as the Screaming Woman , this is bound to happen more often than not. A subtrope of Stock Sound Effect , and an audio variation on Stock Footage . Also common in video games, where it usually becomes annoying. Compare Stock Scream , which denotes a stock sound effect that's commonly plastered in media. If this trope is overused, it can become an Audio Mixing Failure .
